summary: @vpms/design-system exfiltrates host info to remote endpoint on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` script (`node index.js`) unconditionally collects the installer's hostname, current working directory, platform/arch/Node version, package name/version, and the top running processes (via `execSync('ps -eo pid,pcpu,pmem,user,comm --sort=-pcpu | head -n 6')`) and POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to a hardcoded Pipedream endpoint at `https://eov0bmnid410yqf.m.pipedream.net`. The package description self-labels as 'PenTest design system' but the beacon fires on every install regardless of caller, with no opt-in. The `@vpms/design-system` name + scope pattern is consistent with a dependency-confusion attempt against an internal `@vpms` namespace, where any build referencing that internal name would resolve to this public package and beacon the host to the author-controlled endpoint. Installers receive no functional design-system code in return; the only effect is install-time exfiltration of host fingerprinting data.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
